documentation_gaps.title: Create comprehensive CONTRIBUTING.md guide
documentation_gaps.description: The project lacks a CONTRIBUTING.md file which is essential for onboarding new contributors. There's no documentation on pull request process, coding standards, commit message conventions, code review criteria, or community guidelines.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Open source projects and team collaborations need clear contribution guidelines. Without this, new contributors don't know workflows, PR review criteria, or community standards. The CLAUDE.md has good technical guidance but it's AI-focused, not human contributor-focused. A CONTRIBUTING.md is standard practice and expected by developers.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: Only CLAUDE.md exists with development commands, but it's focused on AI agent workflows rather than human contributors. No PR templates or contribution guidelines exist.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Create CONTRIBUTING.md with sections: Getting Started, Development Workflow, Pull Request Process, Code Style Guidelines (Effective Dart), Testing Requirements, Commit Message Conventions, Code Review Criteria, Reporting Bugs, Feature Requests, Community Guidelines. Include .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md and .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/.

documentation_gaps.title: Add architecture diagrams and data flow visualizations
documentation_gaps.description: The ARCHITECTURE.md document is comprehensive but text-only. No visual diagrams exist showing layer relationships, data flow, component interactions, or authentication flow. Complex flows like token refresh, offline queue processing, and state management lack visual representations.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Visual diagrams significantly improve understanding of complex systems. SoloAdventurer has intricate flows: Clean Architecture layers, Riverpod state management, AWS Cognito authentication with token lifecycle, offline-first operation queue, background services. Text descriptions alone make it difficult to grasp the big picture and trace data flow through the system.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: ARCHITECTURE.md has detailed text descriptions and code examples but no diagrams. AUTH_ARCHITECTURE.md explains token flow textually but lacks sequence diagrams.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Create docs/DIAGRAMS.md with architecture visualizations: 1) High-level system architecture diagram (layers, components), 2) Authentication flow sequence diagram (login, token refresh, logout), 3) Offline queue operation flow, 4) Riverpod state management flow, 5) Data flow diagrams for key features, 6) Component interaction diagrams. Use Mermaid or similar tool for maintainable diagrams.

documentation_gaps.title: Create security best practices and hardening guide
documentation_gaps.description: The app implements comprehensive security (AES-256-GCM encryption, PBKDF2 key derivation, secure token storage, constant-time comparisons, rate limiting, key rotation) but no centralized security documentation. Developers modifying security-critical code lack guidance on security principles and threat modeling.
documentation_gaps.rationale: EncryptionService has 423 lines implementing sophisticated security: key rotation, memory locking, rate limiting, MAC verification, PBKDF2 with 100,000 iterations. Auth system handles token blacklisting, suspicious activity detection, audit logging. Without security documentation, developers might inadvertently introduce vulnerabilities when modifying these systems.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: Minimal inline comments in security code. No security design document or threat model. EncryptionService has implementation comments but no security rationale documentation.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Create docs/SECURITY_GUIDE.md with: 1) Security architecture overview (encryption at rest, in transit), 2) Token lifecycle security considerations, 3) Key management strategy (rotation, storage, backup), 4) Threat model and mitigations, 5) Secure coding guidelines, 6) Security dos and don'ts, 7) Incident response procedures, 8) Security testing requirements.

documentation_gaps.title: Create comprehensive error handling and exception guide
documentation_gaps.description: The app defines 23 AuthErrorType values and multiple exception classes (AppException, ServerException, AuthException, ValidationException) but no guide on error handling patterns, recovery strategies, or user-friendly error messages. Developers must read source code to understand error scenarios.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Robust error handling is critical for UX. Auth errors include: UserNotConfirmed, InvalidPassword, TokenExpired, NotAuthorized, LimitExceeded, etc. Each requires different handling (user actions, retries, reauthentication). Without documentation, developers implement inconsistent error handling, leading to poor UX and debugging difficulties.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: Exception types are defined with basic comments. No recovery strategies, user-facing messages, or handling patterns documented.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Create docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md with: 1) Exception hierarchy and when to use each type, 2) Auth error types with recovery strategies, 3) User-friendly error message templates, 4) Error handling patterns in Riverpod (try-catch, AsyncValue.error), 5) Logging and monitoring integration, 6) Testing error scenarios, 7) Common pitfalls and solutions.

documentation_gaps.title: Add detailed inline documentation for complex algorithms
documentation_gaps.description: Several files implement complex algorithms with minimal documentation: TokenManager's adaptive refresh strategy (70-80% of token lifetime), EncryptionService's PBKDF2 key derivation and MAC verification, SessionManager's background refresh scheduling, and the exponential backoff with jitter implementation.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Complex algorithms need clear explanations of: what problem they solve, why this approach was chosen, how they work, edge cases handled, and parameters used. For example, adaptive token refresh uses 70-80% based on AWS best practices, but the rationale isn't documented. Understanding this is crucial for maintenance and debugging.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: Basic class and method comments exist but lack detailed algorithm explanations. TokenManager has debug prints but no explanatory comments about adaptive refresh strategy or backoff logic.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Add comprehensive inline documentation for complex algorithms: 1) Adaptive token refresh strategy (explain 70/75/80% thresholds, AWS rationale), 2) Exponential backoff with jitter (formula, jitter purpose, AWS compatibility), 3) PBKDF2 key derivation (iterations, salt, security tradeoffs), 4) MAC verification for integrity (constant-time comparison, tamper detection), 5) State machine transitions (FeatureAvailability states, triggers). Include references to AWS docs and security best practices.

metadata.notes: Focused on documentation gaps that complement existing docs/README.md, CLAUDE.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md. Prioritized: 1) Missing standard docs (CONTRIBUTING.md), 2) Security-critical areas needing guidance, 3) Visual representations for complex architecture, 4) Error handling consistency, 5) Complex algorithm maintainability. Avoided duplication with existing Getting Started, Troubleshooting, and Feature Tutorial ideas.
